member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMX.AnalyzeIQ1Waveform(System.String,System.String,NationalInstruments.ComplexWaveform{NationalInstruments.ComplexSingle},System.Boolean,System.Int64)
member.summary:
Performs the enabled measurements on the I/Q complex waveform that you specify in IQ parameter. Call this method after you configure the signal and measurement methods. You can fetch measurement results using the Fetch methods or result methods in the property node. Use this method only if the Recommended Acquisition Type method value is either IQ or IQorSpectral .
When using the Analysis-Only mode in RFmxWLAN, the RFmx driver ignores the RFmx hardware settings such as reference level and attenuation. The only RF hardware settings that are not ignored are the center frequency and trigger type, since it is needed for spectral measurement traces as well as some measurements such as ModAcc, ACP, and SEM.
Query the Recommended Acquisition Type method from the RFmxInstr Property Node after calling the RFmx WLAN Commit method.
member.param:
Specifies the result name. The result name can either be specified through this input or the resultName parameter. If you do not specify the result name in this parameter, either the result name specified by the resultName parameter or the default result instance is used. Example:
""
""
"result::r1"
You can use the
method to build the selector string .

Specifies the name to be associated with measurement results. Provide a unique name, such as "r1" to enable fetching of multiple measurement results and traces. This input accepts the result name with or without the "result::" prefix. Example:
"result::r1"
"r1"
Specifies the data for a complex waveform including the start, delta, and actual values.
Resets measurement averaging. If you enable averaging, set this parameter to TRUE for first record and FALSE for subsequent records.
Reserved for future use. Any value passed to this parameter will be ignored.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMX.ConfigureDigitalEdgeTrigger(System.String,System.String,NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDigitalEdgeTriggerEdge,System.Double,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Configures the device to wait for a digital edge trigger and then marks a reference point within the record.
On a MIMO session, this VI configures the digital edge trigger on the master port. By default, the selected port configured to segment0/chain0 is considered as master port.
Spectral measurements are sometimes implemented with multiple acquisitions and therefore will require that digital triggers are sent for each acquisition. Multiple factors, including the desired span versus the realtime bandwidth of the hardware, affect the number of acquisitions. RFmx recommends repeating the generation until the measurement is completed in order to ensure that all the acquisitions are triggered.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.

Specifies the source terminal for the
. This parameter is used only when you set the Trigger Type method to Digital Edge. The default value of this method is hardware dependent.
To make a specific MIMO port as the trigger master, use the port specifier format port::(device_name)/(source_terminal). Example: “port::myrfsa1/PFI0”
Specifies the trigger edge to detect.
Specifies the trigger delay time. This value is expressed in seconds.
Specifies whether to enable the trigger.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.summary:
Configures the device to wait for the complex power of the I/Q data to cross the specified threshold to mark a reference point within the record.
On a MIMO session, configures the IQ Power edge trigger on the master port. By default, the selected port configured to segment0/chain0 is considered as master port.
To trigger on bursty signals, specify a minimum quiet time, which ensures that the trigger does not occur in the middle of the burst signal. The quiet time must be set to a value smaller than the time between bursts, but large enough to ignore power changes within a burst.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.

Specifies the channel from which the device monitors the trigger.This parameter is used only when you set the Trigger Type method to IQ Power Edge. The default value of this method is hardware dependent.
To make a specific MIMO port as the trigger master, use the port specifier format port::(device_name)/(source_terminal). Example: “port::myrfsa1/0”

Specifies whether the device asserts the trigger when the signal power is rising or when it is falling. The device asserts the trigger when the signal power exceeds the specified level with the slope you specify.

Specifies the power level at which the device triggers, depending on the value of the iqPowerEdgeTriggerSlope parameter. The value is expressed in dB when the iqPowerEdgeTriggerLevelType parameter is set to
, or in dBm when it is set to
.
Specifies the trigger delay time. This value is expressed in seconds.
Specifies whether the measurement computes the minimum quiet time used for triggering.

Specifies the duration for which the signal must be quiet before the signal analyzer arms the I/Q power edge trigger. This value is expressed in seconds. If you set
to
, the signal is quiet when it is below the trigger level.

Specifies whether the IQPowerEdgeLevel is set to
or
. The value is expressed in dB when this parameter is set to
. The value is expressed in dBm when this parameter is set to
.
Specifies whether the trigger is used.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMX.AutoLevel(System.String,System.Double)
member.summary:
Examines the input signal to calculate the peak power level and sets it as the value of the
method. Use this method to help calculate an approximate setting for the reference level.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.

Specifies the acquisition length. Use this value to compute the number of samples to acquire from the signal analyzer. This value is expressed in seconds. Auto Level method does not use any trigger for acquisition. It ignores the user-configured trigger methods. NI recommends that you set a sufficiently high measurement interval to ensure that the acquired waveform is at least as long as one period of the signal.
member.remarks:
The RFmxWLAN Auto Level method does the following:
Resets the mixer level, mixer level offset and IF output power offset.
Sets the starting reference level to the maximum reference level supported by the device based on the current RF attenuation, mechanical attenuation and preamp enabled settings.
Iterates to adjust the reference level based on the input signal peak power.
Uses immediate triggering and restores the trigger settings back to user setting after completing execution.
You can also specify the starting reference level using the
method.
When using NI-PXie 5663, NI-PXie 5665, or NI-PXie 5668R devices, NI recommends that you set an appropriate value for mechanical attenuation before calling the RFmxWLAN Auto Level method. Setting an appropriate value for mechanical attenuation reduces the number of times the attenuator settings are changed by this method, thus reducing wear and tear, and maximizing the life time of the attenuator.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

Sets the instrument port to be configured to acquire a signal. Use RFmxInstr_GetAvailablePorts function to get the valid port names.
On a MIMO session, this method specifies one of the initialized devices. Use "port::deviceName/channelNumber" as the format for the selected port. To perform a MIMO measurement, you must configure the selected ports method for the configured number of segments and chains.
For PXIe-5830/5831/5832 devices on a MIMO session, the selected port includes the instrument port in the format "port::deviceName/channelNumber/instrPort".
Example:port::myrfsa1/0/if1

attribute.The default value is 0.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMX.GetOfdmPreamblePuncturingBitmap(System.String,System.Int64@)
member.summary:
Gets the punctured 20 MHz sub-channels in the 802.11ax MU PPDU, the 802.11be MU PPDU or the 802.11bn MU PPDU signal when preamble puncturing is enabled.
The binary representation of the signed integer is interpreted as the bitmap, where a '0' bit
indicates that the corresponding sub-channel is punctured. In the binary representation, the least significant
bit (LSB) maps to the 20 MHz sub-channel lower in frequency, and the most significant bit (MSB) maps
to the 20 MHz sub-channel higher in frequency. For a 80+80 MHz PPDU, the LSB represents the lowest sub-channel
in the lower frequency segment. The puncturing information for the 20 MHz sub-channels of a 80 MHz PPDU
are encoded in the least significant four bits. The puncturing information for the 20 MHz sub-channels
of a 80+80 MHz PPDU or a 160 MHz PPDU is encoded in the least significant eight bits. The puncturing
information for the 20 MHz sub-channels of a 320 MHz PPDU is encoded in the least significant sixteen bits.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.
Upon return, contains the punctured 20 MHz sub-channels in the 802.11ax MU PPDU, the 802.11be MU PPDU or the 802.11bn MU PPDU signal when preamble puncturing is enabled.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.
member.remarks:
This method gets the value of
attribute.The default value is 0xFFF FFFF FFFF FFFF, indicating that none of the eight 20 MHz sub-channels of a 160 MHz PPDU are punctured. The most significant 52 bits are reserved for future use.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMX.SetOfdmPreamblePuncturingBitmap(System.String,System.Int64)
member.summary:
Sets the punctured 20 MHz sub-channels in the 802.11ax MU PPDU, the 802.11be MU PPDU or the 802.11bn MU PPDU signal when preamble puncturing is enabled.
The binary representation of the signed integer is interpreted as the bitmap, where a '0' bit
indicates that the corresponding sub-channel is punctured. In the binary representation, the least significant
bit (LSB) maps to the 20 MHz sub-channel lower in frequency, and the most significant bit (MSB) maps
to the 20 MHz sub-channel higher in frequency. For a 80+80 MHz PPDU, the LSB represents the lowest sub-channel
in the lower frequency segment. The puncturing information for the 20 MHz sub-channels of a 80 MHz PPDU
are encoded in the least significant four bits. The puncturing information for the 20 MHz sub-channels
of a 80+80 MHz PPDU or a 160 MHz PPDU is encoded in the least significant eight bits. The puncturing
information for the 20 MHz sub-channels of a 320 MHz PPDU is encoded in the least significant sixteen bits.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.
Specifies the punctured 20 MHz sub-channels in the 802.11ax MU PPDU or the 802.11be MU PPDU signal when preamble puncturing is enabled.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.
member.remarks:
This method sets the value of
attribute.The default value is 0xFFF FFFF FFFF FFFF, indicating that none of the eight 20 MHz sub-channels of a 160 MHz PPDU are punctured. The most significant 52 bits are reserved for future use.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMX.SetSampleClockRateFactor(System.String,System.Double)
member.summary:
Sets the factor by which the sample clock rate is multiplied at the transmitter to generate a signal compressed in the frequency domain and expanded in the time domain.
For example, a 40 MHz signal can be compressed to 20 MHz in the frequency domain if the sample clock rate is reduced to half at the transmitter. In this case, you must set this method to 0.5 to demodulate the signal.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.
Specifies the factor by which the sample clock rate is multiplied at the transmitter to generate a signal compressed in the frequency domain and expanded in the time domain.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.
member.remarks:
This method sets the value of
attribute.The default value is 1. The valid values are 0.001 to 1, inclusive.

method to build the selector string. If number of segments is greater than 1, you can use the

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMX.ConfigureReferenceLevel(System.String,System.Double)
member.summary: Configures the reference level, which represents the maximum expected power of an RF input signal. On a MIMO session, use "port::(deviceName)/(channelNumber)" as the selector string to configure reference level for each port.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of port string. Example:
""
""
"port::myrfsa1/0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string. On a MIMO session, you can use the RFmxInstr_BuildPortString method to build the selector string.

Specifies the reference level which represents the maximum expected power of the RF input signal. This value is expressed in dBm for RF devices and Vpk-pk for baseband devices. The default value of this method is hardware dependent.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMX.ConfigureExternalAttenuation(System.String,System.Double)
member.summary:
Specifies the attenuation of a switch (or cable) connected to the RF IN connector of the signal analyzer. On a MIMO session, use port::(deviceName)/(channelNumber) as a selector string to configure external attenuation for each port.
On a MIMO session, use port::(deviceName)/(channelNumber) as a selector string to configure or read this property per port. If you do not specify port string, this method is configured for all ports. Refer to the Selector Strings topic for information about the string syntax for named signals.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of port string. Example:
""
""
"port::myrfsa1/0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string. On a MIMO session, you can use the RFmxInstr_BuildPortString method to build the selector string.

Specifies the attenuation of a switch (or cable) connected to the RF IN connector of the signal analyzer. This value is expressed in dB. For more information about attenuation, refer to the Attenuation and Signal Levels topic for your device in the NI RF Vector Signal Analyzers Help .
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataDecodingEnabled.False
member.summary: Disables data decoding.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataDecodingEnabled.True
member.summary: Enables data decoding.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataModulationFormat
member.summary: Returns the data modulation format results of the analyzed waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataModulationFormat.Dsss1Mbps
member.summary: Indicates that the modulation format is DSSS and the data rate is 1 Mbps.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataModulationFormat.Dsss2Mbps
member.summary: Indicates that the modulation format is DSSS and the data rate is 2 Mbps.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataModulationFormat.Cck5_5Mbps
member.summary: Indicates that the modulation format is CCK and the data rate is 5.5 Mbps.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataModulationFormat.Cck11Mbps
member.summary: Indicates that the modulation format is CCK and the data rate is 11 Mbps.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataModulationFormat.Pbcc5_5Mbps
member.summary: Indicates that the modulation format is PBCC and the data rate is 5.5 Mbps.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataModulationFormat.Pbcc11Mbps
member.summary: Indicates that the modulation format is PBCC and the data rate is 11 Mbps.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataModulationFormat.Pbcc22Mbps
member.summary: Indicates that the modulation format is PBCC and the data rate is 22 Mbps.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccDataModulationFormat.Pbcc33Mbps
member.summary: Indicates that the modulation format is PBCC and the data rate is 33 Mbps.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccEqualizationEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable equalization. The IEEE Standard 802.11-2016 does not allow equalization for computing EVM. If you enable equalization, the measurement does not support I/Q impairment estimation.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccEqualizationEnabled.False
member.summary: Disables equalization.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccEqualizationEnabled.True
member.summary: Enables equalization.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccEvmUnit
member.summary: Specifies the unit for the EVM results.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccEvmUnit.Percentage
member.summary: Returns the EVM results as a percentage.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccEvmUnit.dB
member.summary: Returns the EVM results in dB.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccFrequencyErrorCorrectionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable frequency error correction.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccFrequencyErrorCorrectionEnabled.False
member.summary: Disables frequency error correction.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccFrequencyErrorCorrectionEnabled.True
member.summary: Enables frequency error correction.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccIQOriginOffsetCorrectionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable I/Q origin offset correction.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccIQOriginOffsetCorrectionEnabled.False
member.summary: Disables the I/Q origin offset correction.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccIQOriginOffsetCorrectionEnabled.True
member.summary: Enables the I/Q origin offset correction.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPayloadHeaderCrcStatus
member.summary: Returns whether the header cyclic redundancy check (CRC) is successfully passed, as defined in section 16.2.3.7 of IEEE Standard 802.11 2016 .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPayloadHeaderCrcStatus.Fail
member.summary: Returns that the header CRC failed.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPayloadHeaderCrcStatus.Pass
member.summary: Returns that the header CRC passed.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPowerMeasurementEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether power measurement is performed. This measurement computes power of various fields in the PPDU.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPowerMeasurementEnabled.False
member.summary: Disables power measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPowerMeasurementEnabled.True
member.summary: Enables power measurement.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPreambleType
member.summary: Returns the detected preamble type of the acquired burst.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPreambleType.Long
member.summary: Indicates that the PPDU has a long PHY preamble and header.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPreambleType.Short
member.summary: Indicates that the PPDU has a short PHY preamble and header.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPsduCrcStaus
member.summary: Indicates whether the cyclic redundancy check (CRC) of the received decoded PLCP service data unit (PSDU) has passed.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPsduCrcStaus.Fail
member.summary: Indicates that the PSDU CRC failed.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPsduCrcStaus.Pass
member.summary: Indicates that the PSDU CRC passed.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPulseShapingFilterType
member.summary:
Specifies the type of pulse shaping filter used at the transmitter. This method is ignored when you set the
method to
.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPulseShapingFilterType.Rectangular
member.summary: Specifies that the transmitter uses a rectangular pulse shaping filter. The measurement uses an impulse response as the matched filter.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPulseShapingFilterType.RaisedCosine
member.summary: Specifies that the transmitter uses a raised cosine pulse shaping filter. The measurement uses an impulse response as the matched filter.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPulseShapingFilterType.RootRaisedCosine
member.summary: Specifies that the transmitter uses a root raised cosine pulse shaping filter. The measurement uses a root raised cosine filter as the matched filter.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccPulseShapingFilterType.Gaussian
member.summary: Specifies that the transmitter uses a gaussian pulse shaping filter. The measurement uses a gaussian filter as the matched filter.

member.summary: Fetches the EVM results for the DSSSModAcc measurement.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
""
""
"result::r1"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the RMS EVM results of the burst. When you set the
method to
, this result returns the mean of the RMS EVM computed for each averaging count. When you set the
method to
, the measurement returns this result as a percentage. When you set the DSSSModAcc EVM Unit method to
, the measurement returns this result in dB.

Upon return, contains the peak EVM results of the burst. When you set the
method to
, the measurement returns this result as a percentage. When you set the DSSSModAcc EVM Unit method to
, the measurement returns this result in dB. This measurement is performed in accordance with section 16.3.7.9 of IEEE Standard 802.11-2016 . When you set the DSSSModAcc Averaging Enabled method to
, this result returns the maximum of the peak EVM computed for each averaging count.

Upon return, contains the peak EVM results of the burst. When you set the
method to
, the measurement returns this result as a percentage. When you set the DSSSModAcc EVM Unit method to
, the measurement returns this result in dB. This measurement is performed in accordance with section 18.4.7.8 of IEEE Standard 802.11-2007 . When you set the DSSSModAcc Averaging Enabled method to
, this result returns the maximum of the peak EVM computed for each averaging count.

Upon return, contains the peak EVM results of the burst. This value is expressed as a percentage or in dB. This measurement is performed in accordance with section 18.4.7.8 of IEEE Standard 802.11b-1999. When you set the
method to
, this result returns the maximum of the peak EVM computed for each averaging count.

Upon return, contains the carrier frequency error of the transmitter. This value is expressed in Hz. When you set the
method to
, this result returns the mean of the carrier frequency error results computed for each averaging count.

Upon return, contains the chip clock error result of the transmitter. This value is expressed in parts per million (ppm). When you set the DSSSModAcc Averaging Enabled method to
, this result returns the mean of the chip clock error computed for each averaging count.
Upon return, contains the number of chips used for the DSSSModAcc measurement.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXDsssModAccResults.FetchIQImpairments(System.String,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Fetches the I/Q Impairment results for the DSSSModAcc measurement.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
""
""
"result::r1"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the I/Q origin offset. This value is expressed in dB. I/Q origin offset is the ratio of the mean value of the signal to the RMS value of the signal. When you set the
method to
, this parameter returns the mean of the I/Q origin offset results computed for each averaging count.

Upon return, contains the I/Q gain imbalance results. This value is expressed in dB. I/Q gain imbalance is the ratio of the mean amplitude of the in-phase (I) signal to the mean amplitude of the quadrature-phase (Q) signal. When you set the DSSSModAcc Averaging Enabled method to
, this parameter returns the mean of the I/Q gain imbalance results computed for each averaging count.

Upon return, contains the I/Q quadrature error. This value is expressed in degrees. Quadrature error is the deviation in angle from 90 degrees between the in-phase (I) and quadrature-phase (Q) signals. When the DSSSModAcc Averaging Enabled method is set to
, this parameter returns the mean of the I/Q quadrature error results computed for each averaging count.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXLimitedConfigurationChange
member.summary: Specifies the set of properties that are considered by RFmx in the locked signal configuration state.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXLimitedConfigurationChange.Disabled
member.summary: This is the normal mode of RFmx operation. All configuration changes in RFmxInstr properties or in personality properties will be applied during RFmx Commit.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXLimitedConfigurationChange.NoChange
member.summary: Signal configuration and RFmxInstr configuration are locked after the first Commit or Initiate of the named signal configuration. Any configuration change thereafter either in RFmxInstr properties or personality properties will not be considered by subsequent RFmx Commits or Initiates of this signal. Use No Change if you have created named signal configurations for all measurement configurations but are setting some RFmxInstr properties. Refer to the Limitations of the Limited Configuration Change Property topic for more details about the limitations of using this mode.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXLimitedConfigurationChange.Frequency
member.summary:
Signal configuration, other than center frequency, external attenuation, and RFInstr configuration, is locked after first Commit or Initiate of the named signal configuration. Thereafter, only the Center Frequency and
method value changes will be considered by subsequent driver Commits or Initiates of this signal. Refer to the Limitations of the Limited Configuration Change Property topic for more details about the limitations of using this mode.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXLimitedConfigurationChange.ReferenceLevel
member.summary:
Signal configuration, other than the reference level and RFInstr configuration, is locked after first Commit or Initiate of the named signal configuration. Thereafter only the
method value change will be considered by subsequent driver Commits or Initiates of this signal. If you have configured this signal to use an IQ Power Edge Trigger, NI recommends that you set the
to
so that the trigger level is automatically adjusted as you adjust the reference level. Refer to the Limitations of the Limited Configuration Change Property topic for more details about the limitations of using this mode.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXLimitedConfigurationChange.FrequencyAndReferenceLevel
member.summary:
Signal configuration, other than center frequency, reference level, external attenuation, and RFInstr configuration, is locked after first Commit or Initiate of the named signal configuration. Thereafter only Center Frequency, Reference Level, and External Attenuation method value changes will be considered by subsequent driver Commits or Initiates of this signal. If you have configured this signal to use an IQ Power Edge Trigger, NI recommends you set the IQ Power Edge Level Type to
so that the trigger level is automatically adjusted as you adjust the reference level. Refer to the Limitations of the Limited Configuration Change Property topic for more details about the limitations of using this mode.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXLimitedConfigurationChange.SelectedPortsFrequencyAndReferenceLevel
member.summary:

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccAveragingEnabled.True
member.summary:
The measurement uses the value of the
method as the number of acquisitions over which the results are averaged.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccBurstStartDetectionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether the measurement detects a rising edge of a burst in the acquired waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccBurstStartDetectionEnabled.False
member.summary: Disables detecting a rising edge of a burst in the acquired waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccBurstStartDetectionEnabled.True
member.summary: Enables detecting a rising edge of a burst in the acquired waveform.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccCalibrationDataValid
member.summary: Returns whether the calibration data is valid.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccCalibrationDataValid.False
member.summary: Calibration data is not present for the specified configuration or the difference between the current device temperature and the calibration temperature exceeds the [-5 °C, 5 °C] range.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccCalibrationDataValid.True
member.summary: The calibration data is present for the configuration specified by the signal name in the Selector string method.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationInterpolationType
member.summary: Specifies the interpolation type and/or smoothing type used on the channel estimates.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationInterpolationType.Linear
member.summary: Linear interpolation is performed on reference channel estimates across subcarriers.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationInterpolationType.TriangularSmoothing
member.summary: Channel estimates are smoothed using a triangular weighted moving average window across subcarriers.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationInterpolationType.WienerFilter
member.summary: Wiener filter is used for interpolation and smoothing on reference channel estimates across subcarriers.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationLLtfEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to use the legacy channel estimation field for combining with the reference channel frequency response.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationLLtfEnabled.False
member.summary: Channel estimation on L-LTF is disabled.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationLLtfEnabled.True
member.summary: Channel estimation on L-LTF is enabled.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationSmoothingEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether smoothing of the channel estimates is performed using a triangular-weighted moving average window across subcarriers.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationSmoothingEnabled.False
member.summary: Channel smoothing is disabled.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationSmoothingEnabled.True
member.summary: Channel smoothing is enabled.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationType
member.summary: Specifies the fields in the PPDU used to estimate the channel frequency response.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationType.Reference
member.summary: The channel is estimated using long training fields (LTFs) in the preamble and the most recently received midamble, if present.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationType.ReferenceAndData
member.summary: The channel is estimated using long training fields (LTFs) in the preamble, the midamble (if present), and the data field.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationType.ChannelEstimationReference
member.summary: missing content

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelEstimationType.ChannelEstimationReferenceAndData
member.summary: missing content

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelMatrixPowerEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether the channel frequency response matrix power measurements are enabled. This enables cross-power measurements for MIMO signals and user-power measurements for MU signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelMatrixPowerEnabled.False
member.summary: Channel frequency response matrix power measurements are disabled.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccChannelMatrixPowerEnabled.True
member.summary: Channel frequency response matrix power measurements are enabled.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccCommonClockSourceEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether the transmitter uses the same reference clock signal for generating the RF carrier and the symbol clock.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccCommonClockSourceEnabled.False
member.summary: Specifies that the transmitter does not use a common reference clock. The OFDMModAcc measurement computes the symbol clock error and carrier frequency error independently.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccCommonClockSourceEnabled.True
member.summary: Specifies that the transmitter uses a common reference clock. The OFDMModAcc measurement derives the symbol clock error from the configured center frequency and carrier frequency error.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccConfiguration.ConfigureAmplitudeTrackingEnabled(System.String,NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccAmplitudeTrackingEnabled)
member.summary: Configures whether to enable pilot-based mean amplitude tracking per OFDM data symbol.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.

Specifies whether to enable pilot-based mean amplitude tracking per OFDM data symbol. Amplitude tracking is useful if the mean amplitude of the OFDM symbols in a PPDU varies over time. However, enabling tracking may degrade EVM because of attempts to track random amplitude distortions caused by additive noise and other distortions.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.

Specifies whether to enable pilot-based common phase error correction per OFDM data symbol. Phase tracking is useful for tracking the phase variation over the modulation symbol caused by the residual frequency offset and phase noise.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccConfiguration.ConfigureOptimizeDynamicRangeForEvm(System.String,NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccOptimizeDynamicRangeForEvmEnabled,System.Double)
member.summary:
Specifies whether to optimize analyzer’s dynamic range for the EVM measurement.
supporteddevices: PXIe-5646/5840/5841/5842/5860.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.

Specifies whether to optimize the analyzer’s dynamic range for the EVM measurement. This parameter computes optimum attenuation settings for the analyzer based on the reference level you specify while still avoiding ADC or onboard signal processing (OSP) overflow. When you specify the reference level and you notice an overflow error, you can increase the reference level or specify a margin above the reference level by configuring the optimizeDynamicRangeforEVMMargin parameter.

Specifies the margin above the reference level you specify when you set the optimizeDynamicRangeforEVMEnabled parameter to
. This value is expressed in dB. When the parameter's value is 0, the dynamic range is optimized. When you set a positive value to the parameter, the dynamic range reduces from the optimized dynamic range. You can use this parameter to avoid ADC and onboard signal processing (OSP) overflows.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.summary:
Configures the reference waveform for a SISO measurement when you set the
method to
.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.
Specifies the reference waveform used for deriving the reference data symbols for an EVM computation.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccConfiguration.AutoLevel(System.String,System.Double)
member.summary:
Performs the user-configured ModAcc measurement on all initialized devices at multiple reference levels relative to the user-configured method and configures the reference level corresponding to the lowest result on each device.
This method only measures at the reference levels that do not result in an ADC or OSP overflow when you set the method to . If you set the OFDMModAcc Auto Level Allow Overflow method to , this method measures at a few reference levels beyond the overflow.
After calling this method, you need to perform the ModAcc measurement. Calling this method will also set the Reference Level Headroom method to 0.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the EVM results. This value is expressed in seconds. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete.
member.remarks:
This method expects:
A valid OFDMModAcc measurement configuration
method set to peak power of the signal
Repetitive signals at the analyzer's input along with trigger settings that measure the same portion of the waveform every time the measurement is performed
No other measurements are running in parallel
Auto level needs to be performed again if the input signal or RFmx configuration changes.
For repeatable results, you must make sure that the OFDMModAcc measurement is repeatable.
This method measures EVM at reference levels starting at an integer at least 1 dB above the value you configure for the Reference Level method, extending upto 12 dB lower when you set the OFDMModAcc Auto Level Allow Overflow method to , and up to 17 dB lower when you set the OFDMModAcc Auto Level Allow Overflow method to with a step size of 0.5 dB.
When you use this method with the method set to , you need to make sure that valid noise calibration data is available for the above measurements.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccFrequencyErrorEstimationMethod
member.summary: Specifies the PPDU fields that the measurement uses to estimate the carrier frequency error in the acquired signal.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccFrequencyErrorEstimationMethod.Disabled
member.summary: Carrier frequency error is not computed and the corresponding result is returned as NaN.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccFrequencyErrorEstimationMethod.InitialPreamble
member.summary: Initial short and long training fields in the PPDU are used.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccFrequencyErrorEstimationMethod.Preamble
member.summary: Initial short and long training fields along with the SIGnal fields are used.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccFrequencyErrorEstimationMethod.PreambleAndPilots
member.summary: The initial short and long training fields, SIGnal fields, and the pilot subcarriers in the DATA field are used.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccFrequencyErrorEstimationMethod.PreamblePilotsAndData
member.summary: The initial short and long training fields, SIGnal fields, and all the subcarriers in the DATA field are used.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccMeasurementMode
member.summary: Specifies whether the measurement calibrates the noise floor of analyzer or performs the ModAcc measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccMeasurementMode.Measure
member.summary: The OFDMModAcc measurement is performed on the acquired signal.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccMeasurementMode.CalibrateNoiseFloor
member.summary:
The OFDMModAcc measurement measures the noise floor of the instrument across the frequency
range of interest determined by the carrier frequency and channel bandwidth. In this mode, the measurement
expects that the signal generator to be turned off and checks whether no signal power is detected at
the RF In port of the analyzer beyond a certain threshold. All scalar results and traces are invalid
in this mode. Even if the instrument noise floor is previously calibrated, the measurement performs all the required acquisitions and overwrites any pre-existing noise floor calibration data.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccNoiseCompensationEnabled.True

member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
""
""

method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the RMS EVM of all subcarriers in all OFDM symbols. When you set the
method to

Upon return, contains the RMS EVM of data-subcarriers in all OFDM symbols. When you set the

, the measurement returns this result as a percentage. When you set the OFDMModAcc EVM Unit method to
, the measurement returns this result in dB. When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to
, this parameter returns the mean of the composite data RMS EVM results computed for each averaging count.

Upon return, contains the RMS EVM of pilot-subcarriers in all OFDM symbols. When you set the
method to
, the measurement returns this result as a percentage. When you set the OFDMModAcc EVM Unit method to
, the measurement returns this result in dB. When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to
, this parameter returns the mean of the composite pilot RMS EVM results computed for each averaging count.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccResults.FetchChainRmsEvm(System.String,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary:
Fetches the chain RMS EVM results.
Use "segment(n)/chain(k)" as the selector string to read results from this method.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name, segment number, and chain number. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
"segment0/chain0"
"result::r1/segment0/chain0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the chain RMS EVM of all subcarriers in all OFDM symbols. When you set the
method to
, this parameter returns the mean of chain RMS EVM results computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed as a percentage or in dB.

Upon return, contains the chain RMS EVM of data subcarriers in all OFDM symbols. When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to
, this parameter returns the mean of data chain RMS EVM results computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed as a percentage or in dB.

Upon return, contains the chain RMS EVM of pilot subcarriers in all OFDM symbols. When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to
, this parameter returns the mean of pilot chain RMS EVM results computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed as a percentage or in dB.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccResults.FetchIQImpairments(System.String,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.niparametertypes:
System.String,System.Double,out System.Double,out System.Double,out System.Double,out
System.Double,out System.Double
member.summary:
Fetches the I/Q Impairment results for the OFDMModAcc measurement.
Use "segment(n)/chain(k)" as the selector string to read results from this method.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name, segment number, and chain number. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
"segment0/chain0"
"result::r1/segment0/chain0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the relative I/Q origin offset, which is the ratio of the power of the DC subcarrier to the total power of all the subcarriers. When you set the
method to
, this method returns the mean of the relative I/Q origin offset computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed in dB.

Upon return, contains the I/Q gain imbalance, which is the ratio of the RMS amplitude of the in-phase (I) component of the signal to the RMS amplitude of the quadrature-phase (Q) component of the signal. When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to
, this method returns the mean of the I/Q gain imbalance computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed in dB.

Upon return, contains the I/Q quadrature error, which is a measure of deviation of the phase difference between the quadrature-phase (Q) and the in-phase (I) component of the signal from 90 degrees. When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to
, this method returns the I/Q quadrature error computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed in degrees.

Upon return, contains the absolute I/Q origin offset, which is the power of the DC subcarrier. When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to
, this method returns the mean of the absolute I/Q origin offset computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed in dBm.

Upon return, contains the I/Q timing skew, which is the difference between the group delay of the in-phase (I) and quadrature (Q) components of the signal. When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to
, this method returns the mean of the I/Q timing skew computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed in seconds.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccResults.FetchSpectralFlatness(System.String,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Int32@)
member.summary:
Fetches the spectral flatness margin results.
Use "segment(n)/chain(k)/stream(l)" as the selector string to read results from this method.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name, segment number, chain number, and stream number. Example:
"segment0/chain0/stream0"
"result::r1/segment0/chain0/stream0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the spectral flatness margin, which is the minimum of the upper and lower spectral flatness margins. The upper spectral flatness margin is the minimum difference between the upper mask and the spectral flatness across subcarriers. The lower spectral flatness margin is the minimum difference between the spectral flatness and the lower mask across subcarriers. When you set the
method to
, the spectral flatness is computed using the mean of the channel frequency response magnitude computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed in dB.
Upon return, contains the subcarrier index corresponding to the spectralFlatnessMargin parameter.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccResults.FetchUnusedToneError(System.String,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Int32@)
member.summary:
Fetches the unused tone error margin results.
Refer to Unused Tone Error Measurement for more information.
Use "segment(n)/chain(k)" as the selector string to read results from this method.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name, segment number, and chain number. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
"segment0/chain0"
"result::r1/segment0/chain0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.
Upon return, contains the unused tone error margin, which is the minimum difference between the unused tone error mask and the unused tone error across 26-tone RUs. This value is expressed in dB.
Upon return, contains the 26-tone RU index corresponding to the unusedToneErrorMargin parameter.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the peak power of the PPDU. When you set the
method to
, this parameter returns the maximum of the PPDU peak power results computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed in dBm.

method to build the selector string.

member.summary:
Fetches the RU offset and the RU size of the specified user.
Use "user(n)" as the selector string to read results from this method.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name, and user number. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
"user0"
"result::r1/user0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the location of RU or MRU for a user. If an RU is configured, the RU Offset is in terms of the index of a 26-tone RU, assuming the entire bandwidth is composed of 26-tone RUs. If an MRU is configured, the MRU Index is as defined in the Table 36-8 to Table 36-15 of IEEE P802.11be/D7.0.
Upon return, contains the RU size for the specified user.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.summary:
Fetches the common pilot error magnitude and phase traces.
Use "segment(n)" as the selector string to read results from this method.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name, and segment number. Example:
"segment0"
"result::r1/segment0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the common pilot error magnitude trace. Common pilot error for an OFDM symbol is the correlation of the received pilot subcarrier BPSK symbols with their ideal values. This trace represents the magnitude of the common pilot error for each OFDM symbol.

Upon return, contains the common pilot error phase trace. Common pilot error for an OFDM symbol is the correlation of the received pilot subcarrier BPSK symbols with their ideal values. This trace represents the phase of the common pilot error for each OFDM symbol.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSigBCrcStatus
member.summary: Returns whether the cyclic redundancy check (CRC) has passed for the HE-SIG-B field of the 802.11ax MU PPDU waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSigBCrcStatus.NotApplicable
member.summary: Returns that the SIG-B CRC is invalid for the current waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSigBCrcStatus.Fail
member.summary: Returns that the SIG-B CRC failed.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSigBCrcStatus.Pass
member.summary: Returns that the SIG-B CRC passed.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSigCrcStatus
member.summary: Returns whether the cyclic redundancy check (CRC) has passed either for the HT-SIG field of the 802.11n waveform, for the VHT-SIG-A field of the 802.11ac waveform, or for the HE-SIG-A field of the 802.11ax waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSigCrcStatus.NotApplicable
member.summary: Returns that the SIG CRC is invalid for the current waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSigCrcStatus.Fail
member.summary: Returns that the SIG CRC failed.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSigCrcStatus.Pass
member.summary: Returns that the SIG CRC passed.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSpectrumInverted
member.summary: Specifies whether the spectrum of the measured signal is inverted. The inversion happens when the I and the Q components of the baseband complex signal are swapped.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSpectrumInverted.False
member.summary: The spectrum of the measured signal is not inverted.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSpectrumInverted.True
member.summary: The measured signal is inverted and the measurement corrects the signal by swapping the I and the Q components.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSymbolClockErrorCorrectionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable symbol clock error correction.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSymbolClockErrorCorrectionEnabled.False
member.summary: Symbol clock error correction is disabled.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccSymbolClockErrorCorrectionEnabled.True
member.summary: Symbol clock error correction is enabled.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccUnusedToneErrorMaskReference
member.summary: Specifies the reference used to create the unused tone error mask for the 802.11ax, 802.11be or 802.11bn TB PPDU signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccUnusedToneErrorMaskReference.Limit1
member.summary: Applies the mask corresponding to the case when the transmit power of the DUT is less than or equal to the maximum power of MCS7.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccUnusedToneErrorMaskReference.Limit2
member.summary: Applies the mask corresponding to the case when the transmit power of the DUT is more than the maximum power of MCS7.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccUSigCrcStatus
member.summary: Returns whether the cyclic redundancy check (CRC) has passed for the U-SIG field of the 802.11be or the 802.11bn waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccUSigCrcStatus.NotApplicable
member.summary: Returns that the U-SIG CRC is invalid for the current waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccUSigCrcStatus.Fail
member.summary: Returns that the U-SIG CRC failed.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccUSigCrcStatus.Pass
member.summary: Returns that the U-SIG CRC passed.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPowerRampConfiguration.ConfigureAcquisitionLength(System.String,System.Double)
member.summary: Configures the waveform acquisition length for the measurement.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.

Specifies the duration of the signal to be acquired for the PowerRamp measurement. This value is expressed in seconds. You must set this parameter to a value that is greater than or equal to the duration of the PPDU under analysis, so that the acquired signal contains both rising and falling power ramp transitions.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.niparametertypes:
System.String,System.Double,ref NationalInstruments.AnalogWaveform`1[[System.Single,
mscorlib, Version=4.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b77a5c561934e089]],ref NationalInstruments.AnalogWaveform`1[[System.Single,

mscorlib, Version=4.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b77a5c561934e089]]
member.summary: Returns the raw, processed, threshold and power-reference traces at the beginning of a burst.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
""
""
"result::r1"
You can use the
method to build the selector string .

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.
Upon return, contains the power versus time trace of the acquired waveform at the beginning of the burst.
Upon return, contains the power versus time trace of the power envelope of the acquired waveform at the beginning of the burst.
Upon return, contains the threshold trace indicating the 10 percent and the 90 percent power levels.
Upon return, contains the power reference trace which indicates the 100% power level, corresponding to the steady state average power level of the initial portion of the burst.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.
