member.summary: Returns the raw, processed, thresholding and power reference waveforms at the end of a burst.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
""
""
"result::r1"
You can use the
method to build the selector string .

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.
Upon return, contains the power versus time trace of the acquired waveform at the end of the burst.
Upon return, contains the power versus time trace of the power envelope of the acquired waveform at the end of the burst.
Upon return, contains the threshold trace indicating the 10 percent and the 90 percent power levels.
Upon return, contains the power reference trace which indicates the 100% power level, corresponding to the steady state average power level of the final portion of the burst.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPowerRampResults.FetchMeasurement(System.String,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Returns the PowerRamp rise time and fall time.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
""
""
"result::r1"
You can use the
method to build the selector string .

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the rise time of the acquired signal that is the amount of time taken for the power envelope to rise from a level of 10 percent to 90 percent. This value is expressed in seconds. When you set the
method to
, this parameter returns the mean of the rise time computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains the fall time of the acquired signal that is the amount of time taken for the power envelope to fall from a level of 90 percent to 10 percent. This value is expressed in seconds. When you set the PowerRamp Averaging Enabled method to
, this parameter returns the mean of the fall time computed for each averaging count. This value is expressed in seconds.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId
member.summary: Specifies all the attribute identifiers.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.SelectedPorts
member.summary: Specifies the instrument port to be configured to acquire a signal. Use RFmxInstr_GetAvailablePorts function to get the valid port names.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.CenterFrequency
member.summary:
Specifies the expected carrier frequency of the RF signal that needs to be acquired. This value is expressed in Hz. The signal analyzer tunes to this frequency.
On a MIMO session, use segment(n) along with a named or default signal instance as the selector string to configure this method. Refer to the Selector Strings topic for information about the string syntax for named signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.ReferenceLevel
member.summary:
Specifies the reference level which represents the maximum expected power of the RF input signal. This value is expressed in dBm for RF devices and Vpk-pk for baseband devices.
On a MIMO session, use port::(deviceName)/(channelNumber) as a selector string to configure or read this property per port. If you do not specify port string, this method is configured for all ports. Refer to the Selector Strings topic for information about the string syntax for named signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.ExternalAttenuation
member.summary: Specifies the attenuation of a switch (or cable) connected to the RF IN connector of the signal analyzer. This value is expressed in dB. For more information about attenuation, refer to the Attenuation and Signal Levels topic for your device in the NI RF Vector Signal Analyzers Help .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.ReferenceLevelHeadroom
member.summary:
Specifies the margin RFmx adds to the
method. The margin avoids clipping and overflow warnings if the input signal exceeds the configured reference level.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.TriggerType
member.summary: Specifies the trigger type.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DigitalEdgeTriggerSource
member.summary:
Specifies the source terminal for the digital edge trigger. This method is used only when you set the
method to
.
On a MIMO session, this method configures the digital edge trigger on the master port. By default, the Selected Ports method is configured to "segment0/chain0" and is considered as the master port.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DigitalEdgeTriggerEdge
member.summary:
Specifies the active edge for the trigger. This method is used only when you set the
method to
.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.IQPowerEdgeTriggerSource
member.summary:
Specifies the channel from which the device monitors the trigger. This method is used only when you set the
method to
. On a MIMO session, configures the IQ Power edge trigger on the master port. By default, the selected port configured to segment0/chain0 is considered as master port.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.IQPowerEdgeTriggerLevel
member.summary:
Specifies the power level at which the device triggers. This value is expressed in dB when you set the
method to
and in dBm when you set the IQ Power Edge Level Type method to
.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.IQPowerEdgeTriggerLevelType
member.summary:
Specifies the reference for the
method. The IQ Power Edge Level Type method is used only when you set the
method to
.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.IQPowerEdgeTriggerSlope
member.summary: Specifies whether the device asserts the trigger when the signal power is rising or falling.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.TriggerDelay
member.summary: Specifies the trigger delay time. This value is expressed in seconds.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.TriggerMinimumQuietTimeMode
member.summary: Specifies whether the measurement computes the minimum quiet time used for triggering.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.TriggerMinimumQuietTimeDuration
member.summary: Specifies the time duration for which the signal must be quiet before the signal analyzer arms the I/Q power edge trigger. This value is expressed in seconds.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.TriggerGateEnabled
member.summary: Enables time-domain gating of the acquired signal for SEM measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.TriggerGateLength
member.summary:
Specifies the maximum duration of time for each record used for computing the spectrum when you are performing an SEM measurement and when you set the
method to
.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.Standard
member.summary: Specifies the signal under analysis as defined in IEEE Standard 802.11 .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.ChannelBandwidth
member.summary: Specifies the channel spacing as defined under section 3.1 of IEEE Standard 802.11–2016 (pp. 130) . This value is specified in Hz.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.NumberOfFrequencySegments
member.summary: Specifies the number of frequency segments for 802.11ac and 802.11ax signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.NumberOfReceiveChains
member.summary: Specifies the number of receive chains for OFDM standards.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmFrequencySegmentIndex
member.summary: Specifies the frequency segment index to be analyzed in an 80+80 MHz 802.11ax signal. You must set this method to either of the valid values when you want to analyze one of the two segments.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmTransmitPowerClass
member.summary:
Specifies the STA transmit power classification as defined in annexture D.2.2 of IEEE Standard 802.11–2016 , if you set the
method to
.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmFrequencyBand
member.summary: Specifies the ISM frequency band. The SEM measurement uses this information to select an appropriate mask as defined in IEEE Standard 802.11n – 2009 .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmAutoPpduTypeDetectionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable auto detection of the PPDU type when performing the OFDMModAcc measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmPpduType
member.summary:
Specifies the PPDU type when you set the
method to
.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmHeaderDecodingEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable the decoding of the header fields in the PPDU.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmEhtSigCompressionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable EHT-SIG compression. This method is applicable only for 802.11be MU PPDU signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmNumberOfUsers
member.summary: Specifies the number of users in a multi-user (MU) PPDU.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmMcsIndex
member.summary:
Specifies the modulation and coding scheme (MCS) index or the data rate when you set the
method to
.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmScramblerSeed
member.summary:
Specifies the scrambler seed for combined signal demodulation. This is applicable only if
is set to .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmFecCodingType
member.summary: Specifies the type of forward error correction (FEC) coding used.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmRUSize
member.summary: Specifies the size of the resource unit (RU) or the multiple resource unit (MRU) in terms of number of subcarriers for 802.11ax, 802.11be, and 802.11bn signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmRUOffsetMruIndex
member.summary: Specifies the location of RU or MRU for a user. If an RU is configured, the RU Offset is in terms of the index of a 26-tone RU, assuming the entire bandwidth is composed of 26-tone RUs. If an MRU is configured, the MRU Index is as defined in the Table 36-8 to Table 36-15 of IEEE P802.11be/D7.0 If a dRU is configured, the RU Offset represents dRU Index as defined in the Table 38-4 to Table 38-6 and the Equation 38-1 of IEEE P802.11bn/D1.0 .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmGuardIntervalType
member.summary: Specifies the size of the guard interval of OFDM symbols.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmLtfSize
member.summary:
Specifies the LTF symbol size. This method is applicable only for 802.11ax, 802.11be, and 802.11bn signals.
For 802.11ax Trigger-based PPDU, you must always configure this method. For other signals, you must configure this method, if OFDMHeaderDecodingEnabled is False.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmPreFecPaddingFactor
member.summary: Specifies the pre-FEC padding factor used in 802.11ax TB PPDU, 802.11be TB PPDU and 802.11bn TB PPDU for decoding PLCP service data unit (PSDU) bits.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmLdpcExtraSymbolSegment
member.summary: Specifies the presence of an extra OFDM symbol segment for LDPC in the 802.11ax TB PPDU, 802.11be TB PPDU, and 802.11bn TB PPDU.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmPEDisambiguity
member.summary: Specifies the packet extension disambiguity information.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmStbcEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether space-time block coding is enabled. This method is applicable only for 802.11ax TB PPDU.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmNumberOfSpaceTimeStreams
member.summary: Specifies the number of space time streams.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmSpaceTimeStreamOffset
member.summary: Specifies the space time stream offset.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmNumberOfHESigBSymbols
member.summary: Specifies the number of HE-SIG-B symbols.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmNumberOfEhtSigSymbols
member.summary: Specifies the number of EHT-SIG symbols. This method is applicable only for 802.11be MU PPDU signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmDcmEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether the dual carrier modulation (DCM) is applied to the data field of the 802.11ax TB PPDU signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmNumberOfLtfSymbols
member.summary: Specifies the number of HE-LTF, EHT-LTF, or UHR-LTF symbols in the 802.11ax TB PPDU, 802.11be or 802.11bn TB PPDU, respectively.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmMUMimoLtfModeEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether the LTF sequence corresponding to each space-time stream is masked by a distinct orthogonal code.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmPreamblePuncturingEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether the 802.11ax MU PPDU, the 802.11be MU PPDU or the 802.11bn MU PPDU signal is preamble punctured.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmPreamblePuncturingBitmap
member.summary:
Specifies the punctured 20 MHz sub-channels in the 802.11ax MU PPDU, the 802.11be MU PPDU or the 802.11bn MU PPDU signal when preamble puncturing is enabled.
The binary representation of the signed integer is interpreted as the bitmap, where a '0' bit
indicates that the corresponding sub-channel is punctured. In the binary representation, the least significant
bit (LSB) maps to the 20 MHz sub-channel lower in frequency, and the most significant bit (MSB) maps
to the 20 MHz sub-channel higher in frequency. For a 80+80 MHz PPDU, the LSB represents the lowest sub-channel
in the lower frequency segment. The puncturing information for the 20 MHz sub-channels of a 80 MHz PPDU
are encoded in the least significant four bits. The puncturing information for the 20 MHz sub-channels
of a 80+80 MHz PPDU or a 160 MHz PPDU is encoded in the least significant eight bits. The puncturing
information for the 20 MHz sub-channels of a 320 MHz PPDU is encoded in the least significant sixteen bits.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmAutoPhaseRotationDetectionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable auto detection of phase rotation coefficients.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmPhaseRotationCoefficient1
member.summary: Specifies the phase rotation coefficient 1 as defined in IEEE Standard P802.11be/D7.0 .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmPhaseRotationCoefficient2
member.summary: Specifies the phase rotation coefficient 2 as defined in IEEE Standard P802.11be/D7.0 .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmPhaseRotationCoefficient3
member.summary: Specifies the phase rotation coefficient 3 as defined in IEEE Standard P802.11be/D7.0 .

member.summary:
Returns the standard detected by the
function.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DetectedChannelBandwidth
member.summary:
Returns the channel bandwidth detected by the
. The value is expressed in Hz.

member.summary:
Returns the duration of the packet detected by the

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DsssModAccMeasurementEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable the DSSSModAcc measurement, which is a measurement of the modulation accuracy on signals conforming to the DSSS PHY defined in section 15 and the High Rate DSSS PHY defined in section 16 of IEEE Standard 802.11-2016 .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DsssModAccAcquisitionLengthMode
member.summary: Specifies whether the measurement automatically computes the acquisition length of the waveform based on DSSSModAcc properties.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DsssModAccAcquisitionLength
member.summary:
Specifies the length of the waveform to be acquired for the DSSSModAcc measurement when you set the
method to
. This value is expressed in seconds.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DsssModAccMeasurementOffset
member.summary: Specifies the number of data chips to be ignored from the start of the data field for the EVM computation. This value is expressed in chips.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DsssModAccMaximumMeasurementLength
member.summary: Specifies the maximum number of data chips that the measurement uses to compute EVM. This value is expressed in chips.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DsssModAccPulseShapingFilterType
member.summary:
Specifies the type of pulse shaping filter used at the transmitter. This method is ignored when you set the
method to
.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DsssModAccPulseShapingFilterParameter
member.summary: Specifies the value of the filter roll-off when you set the Pulse Shaping Filter Type method to Raised Cosine or Root Raised Cosine. This method is ignored if you set the Pulse Shaping Filter Type method to Rectangular.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.DsssModAccEqualizationEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable equalization. The IEEE Standard 802.11-2016 does not allow equalization for computing EVM. If you enable equalization, the measurement does not support I/Q impairment estimation.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccMeasurementEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable OFDMModAcc measurement for OFDM based standards.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccAveragingEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable averaging for OFDMModAcc measurements.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccAveragingCount
member.summary:
Specifies the number of acquisitions used for averaging when you set the
method to
.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccAveragingType
member.summary:
Specifies the averaging type for the OFDMModAcc measurement. This method is considered only when you set the
method to True and when you set the
method to a value greater than 1.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccVectorAveragingTimeAlignmentEnabled
member.summary:
Specifies whether to enable time alignment for the acquired I/Q data across multiple acquisitions. This method is considered only when you set the
method to True , when you set the
method to a value greater than 1, and when you set the
method to Vector . You can set this method to False when there is no time offset between the acquired I/Q data of all averaging counts.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccVectorAveragingPhaseAlignmentEnabled
member.summary:
Specifies whether to enable phase alignment for the acquired I/Q data across multiple acquisitions. This method is considered only when you set the
method to True , when you set the
method to a value greater than 1, and when you set the
method to Vector . You can set this method to False when there is no phase offset between the acquired I/Q data of all averaging counts.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccMeasurementMode
member.summary: Specifies whether the measurement calibrates the noise floor of analyzer or performs the ModAcc measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccEvmReferenceDataSymbolsMode

member.summary: Specifies the unit for EVM results.

member.summary:
Specifies the length of the waveform to be acquired for the OFDMModAcc measurement, when you set the

. This value is expressed in seconds.

member.summary: Specifies the number of data symbols to be ignored from the start of the data field for EVM computation. This value is expressed in symbols.

member.summary: Specifies the maximum number of OFDM symbols that the measurement uses to compute EVM. This value is expressed in symbols.

member.summary: Specifies whether to enable demodulation of the signal that is formed by combining signals from multiple transmitter chains.

member.summary: Identifies the reference files used for combined signal demodulation. The value of this method must be same as the value of the Reference Data Identifier string specified while creating the reference files. This is applicable only if is set to .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccBurstStartDetectionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether the measurement detects a rising edge of a burst in the acquired waveform.

member.summary: Specifies whether the transmitter uses the same reference clock signal for generating the RF carrier and the symbol clock.

member.summary: Specifies whether to enable pilot-based mean amplitude tracking per OFDM data symbol.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccPhaseTrackingEnabled

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccSpectrumInverted
member.summary: Specifies whether the spectrum of the measured signal is inverted. The inversion happens when the I and the Q components of the baseband complex signal are swapped.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccChannelEstimationType
member.summary: Specifies the fields in the PPDU used to estimate the channel frequency response.

member.summary: Specifies whether smoothing of the channel estimates is performed using a triangular-weighted moving average window across subcarriers.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccChannelEstimationInterpolationType
member.summary: Specifies the interpolation type and/or smoothing type used on the channel estimates.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccChannelEstimationSmoothingLength
member.summary: Specifies the length of the triangular-weighted moving window across subcarriers that is used for averaging the channel estimate.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccChannelEstimationRelativeDelaySpread
member.summary: Specifies the expected channel delay spread relative to the OFDM symbol length.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccChannelEstimationLLtfEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to use the legacy channel estimation field for combining with the reference channel frequency response.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccPowerCustomGateStopTime
member.summary:
Specifies the stop time of the custom power gate, and must be greater than the corresponding
method. This value is expressed in seconds.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccChannelMatrixPowerEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether the channel frequency response matrix power measurements are enabled. This enables cross-power measurements for MIMO signals and user-power measurements for MU signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccIQImpairmentsEstimationEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable the estimation of I/Q gain imbalance, I/Q quadrature error, and I/Q timing skew impairments.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccIQImpairmentsModel
member.summary: Specifies the I/Q impairments model used by the measurement for estimating I/Q impairments.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccIQGainImbalanceCorrectionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable I/Q gain imbalance correction.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccIQQuadratureErrorCorrectionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable I/Q quadrature error correction.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccIQTimingSkewCorrectionEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable I/Q timing skew correction.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccIQImpairmentsPerSubcarrierEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to estimate I/Q impairments independently for each subcarrier.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccUnusedToneErrorMaskReference
member.summary: Specifies the reference used to create the unused tone error mask for the 802.11ax, 802.11be or 802.11bn TB PPDU signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccDataDecodingEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to decode data bits and check for the validity of the cyclic redundancy check (CRC).

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccNoiseCompensationEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether the contribution of the instrument noise is compensated for EVM computation.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccNoiseCompensationInputPowerCheckEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether the measurement checks if any high power signal is present at the RFIn port of the instrument while performing noise floor calibration.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccNoiseCompensationReferenceLevelCoercionLimit
member.summary: Specifies the reference level coercion limit for noise compensation. This value is expressed in dB.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccOptimizeDynamicRangeForEvmEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to optimize the analyzer's dynamic range for the EVM measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccOptimizeDynamicRangeForEvmMargin
member.summary:
Specifies the margin above the reference level you specify when you set the
method to
. This value is expressed in dB.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccAutoLevelAllowOverflow
member.summary: Specifies whether the function should search for the optimum reference levels while allowing ADC overflow.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccAllTracesEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable all the traces computed by the OFDMModAcc measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccNumberOfAnalysisThreads
member.summary: Specifies the maximum number of threads used for parallelism for the OFDMModAcc measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsCompositeRmsEvmMean
member.summary:
Returns the RMS EVM of all subcarriers in all OFDM symbols. This value is expressed as a percentage or in dB.
When you set OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to True, this method returns the mean of RMS EVM results for the specified user that is computed for each averaging count.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsCompositeDataRmsEvmMean
member.summary:
Returns the RMS EVM of data-subcarriers in all OFDM symbols. This value is expressed as a percentage or in dB.
When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to True, this method returns the mean of data RMS EVM results computed for each averaging count.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsCompositePilotRmsEvmMean
member.summary:
Returns the RMS EVM of pilot-subcarriers in all OFDM symbols. This value is expressed as a percentage or in dB.
When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method is set to True, this method returns the mean of pilot RMS EVM results computed for each averaging count.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsStreamRmsEvmMean
member.summary:
Returns the stream RMS EVM of all subcarriers in all OFDM symbols. This value is expressed as a percentage or in dB.
When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to True, this method returns the mean of Stream RMS EVM results computed for each averaging count.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsUserPowerMean
member.summary:
Returns the user power. User power is the frequency domain power measured over subcarriers occupied by a given user. This value is expressed in dBm.
When you set the OFDMModAccAveragingEnabled method to True, this method returns the mean of chain RMS EVM results computed for each averaging count.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsUnusedToneErrorMarginRUIndex
member.summary:

result.

member.summary: Returns the absolute time corresponding to the detected start of the analyzed burst. The start time is computed with respect to the initial time value of the acquired waveform. This value is expressed in seconds.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsIQGainImbalanceMean
member.summary: Returns the I/Q gain imbalance, which is the ratio of the RMS amplitude of the in-phase (I) component of the signal to the RMS amplitude of the quadrature-phase (Q) component of the signal. This value is expressed in dB.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsIQQuadratureErrorMean
member.summary: Returns the I/Q quadrature error, which is a measure of deviation of the phase difference between the quadrature-phase (Q) and the in-phase (I) component of the signal from 90 degrees. This value is expressed in degrees.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsIQQuadratureErrorMaximum
member.summary: Missing content

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsIQTimingSkewMean
member.summary: Returns the I/Q timing skew, which is the difference between the group delay of the in-phase (I) and quadrature (Q) components of the signal. This value is expressed in seconds.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsRmsCommonPhaseErrorMean
member.summary: Returns the RMS common phase error.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsRmsCommonPilotErrorMean
member.summary: Returns the RMS common pilot error. This value is expressed as a percentage.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsPpduType
member.summary: Returns the PPDU type of the measured signal.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsMcsIndex
member.summary: Returns the MCS index or the data rate of the measured signal.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsAggregation
member.summary: Returns the value of the Aggregation field as decoded from the high-throughput signal (HT-SIG) field of 802.11n signal.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsFecCodingType
member.summary: Returns the FEC coding type for a specified user.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsRUSize
member.summary: Returns the RU or the MRU size.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsRUOffset
member.summary: Returns the location of RU or MRU for a user. If an RU is configured, the RU Offset is in terms of the index of a 26-tone RU, assuming the entire bandwidth is composed of 26-tone RUs. If an MRU is configured, the MRU Index is as defined in Table 36-8 to Table 36-15 of IEEE P802.11be/D7.0 .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsRUOffsetMruIndex
member.summary: Returns the location of RU or MRU for a user. If an RU is configured, the RU Offset is in terms of the index of a 26-tone RU, assuming the entire bandwidth is composed of 26-tone RUs. If an MRU is configured, the MRU Index is as defined in Table 36-8 to Table 36-15 of IEEE P802.11be/D7.0 .

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsNumberOfUsers
member.summary: Returns the number of users.

member.summary: Returns the number of HE-SIG-B symbols.

member.summary: Returns the size of the guard interval of OFDM symbols.

Returns the HE-LTF size, EHT-LTF or UHR-LTF size when you set the

or
or
, respectively.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsNumberOfSpaceTimeStreams
member.summary: Returns the number of space time streams.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsSpaceTimeStreamOffset
member.summary: Returns the space time stream offset. This method is applicable only to 802.11ac, 802.11ax, 802.11be, and 802.11bn signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsDcmEnabled
member.summary: Returns whether DCM is enabled for a specified user.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsLSigParityCheckStatus
member.summary: Returns whether the parity check has passed either for the SIGNAL field of the 802.11a/g waveform or for the L-SIG field of the 802.11n/802.11ac/802.11ax/802.11be/802.11bn waveforms.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsSigCrcStatus
member.summary: Returns whether the cyclic redundancy check (CRC) has passed either for the HT-SIG field of the 802.11n waveform, for the VHT-SIG-A field of the 802.11ac waveform, or for the HE-SIG-A field of the 802.11ax waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsSigBCrcStatus
member.summary: Returns whether the cyclic redundancy check (CRC) has passed for the HE-SIG-B field of the 802.11ax MU PPDU waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsUSigCrcStatus
member.summary: Returns whether the cyclic redundancy check (CRC) has passed for the U-SIG field of the 802.11be or the 802.11bn waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsEhtSigCrcStatus
member.summary: Returns whether the cyclic redundancy check (CRC) has passed for the EHT-SIG field of the 802.11be waveform.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsPsduCrcStatus
member.summary: Indicates whether the cyclic redundancy check (CRC) of the received decoded PLCP service data unit (PSDU) has passed.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsScramblerSeed
member.summary: Returns the detected initial state of the scrambler, which is used to scramble the data bits in the device under test (DUT). RFmx uses the same seed to descramble the received bit-sequence.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.OfdmModAccResultsPEDuration
member.summary: Returns the duration of the packet extension field for the 802.11ax, 802.11be and 802.11bn signals. This value is expressed in seconds.

.

member.summary: Specifies the start frequency of the offset segment relative to the carrier frequency. This value is expressed in Hz.

member.summary: Specifies the stop frequency of the offset segment relative to carrier frequency. This value is expressed in Hz.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.SemOffsetRelativeLimitStop
member.summary: Specifies the relative power limit corresponding to the end of the offset segment. This value is expressed in dB.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.SemSpan
member.summary: Specifies the frequency range of the spectrum used for the SEM measurement. This value is expressed in Hz.

member.summary: Specifies whether the sweep time for the SEM measurement is computed automatically or is configured by you.

member.summary: Specifies whether to enable averaging for the SEM measurement.

.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.SemAveragingType

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.SemAmplitudeCorrectionType
member.summary: Specifies whether the amplitude of the frequency bins, used in the measurement, is corrected for external attenuation at the RF center frequency, or at the individual frequency bins. Use the RFmxInstr_CfgExternalAttenuationTable function to configure the external attenuation table.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.SemAllTracesEnabled

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.SemResultsMeasurementStatus
member.summary: Returns the overall measurement status, indicating whether the spectrum exceeds the SEM measurement mask limits in any of the offset segments.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.SemResultsCarrierAbsoluteIntegratedPower
member.summary:
Returns the average power of the carrier channel over the bandwidth indicated by the
method. This value is expressed in dBm.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXPropertyId.SemResultsCarrierAbsolutePeakPower
member.summary:
Returns the peak power in the carrier channel over the bandwidth indicated by the
method. This value is expressed in dBm. SEM mask level is determined by this result.

member.summary: Returns the frequency at which the peak power occurs in the carrier channel. This value is expressed in Hz.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSem
member.summary: Represents the SEM measurement.

member.name: P:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSem.Configuration
member.summary:
Gets the
instance that provides methods to configure the SEM measurement.

member.name: P:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSem.Results
member.summary:
Gets the
instance that provides methods to fetch and read the SEM measurement results.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAmplitudeCorrectionType
member.summary: Specifies whether the amplitude of the frequency bins, used in the measurement, is corrected for external attenuation at the RF center frequency, or at the individual frequency bins. Use the RFmxInstr_CfgExternalAttenuationTable function to configure the external attenuation table.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAmplitudeCorrectionType.RFCenterFrequency
member.summary: All the frequency bins in the spectrum are compensated with a single external attenuation value that corresponds to the RF center frequency.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAmplitudeCorrectionType.SpectrumFrequencyBin
member.summary: An individual frequency bin in the spectrum is compensated with the external attenuation value corresponding to that frequency.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAveragingEnabled
member.summary: Specifies whether to enable averaging for the SEM measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAveragingEnabled.False
member.summary: The measurement is performed on a single acquisition.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAveragingEnabled.True
member.summary:
The SEM measurement uses the
method as the number of acquisitions over which the SEM measurement is averaged.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAveragingType
member.summary: Specifies the averaging type for averaging multiple spectrum acquisitions. The averaged spectrum is used for SEM measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAveragingType.Rms
member.summary: The power spectrum is linearly averaged. RMS averaging reduces signal fluctuations but not the noise floor.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAveragingType.Log
member.summary: The power spectrum is averaged in a logarithmic scale.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAveragingType.Scalar
member.summary: The square root of the power spectrum is averaged.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAveragingType.Maximum
member.summary: The peak power in the spectrum is retained from one acquisition to the next at each frequency bin.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemAveragingType.Minimum
member.summary: The least power in the spectrum is retained from one acquisition to the next at each frequency bin.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemConfiguration.GetMeasurementEnabled(System.String,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Gets whether to enable the spectral emission mask (SEM) measurement.
member.param: Pass an empty string. The signal name that is passed when creating the signal configuration is used.
Upon return, contains whether to enable the spectral emission mask (SEM) measurement.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.
member.remarks:
This method gets the value of
attribute.The default value is FALSE.

member.remarks:
This method sets the value of
attribute.The default value is FALSE.

member.summary: Returns an array of the absolute and relative carrier power measurements. The relative power is relative to the peak power of the carrier.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
""
""
"result::r1"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.

Upon return, contains an array of average power of the carrier channel over the bandwidth indicated by the
method. This value is expressed in dBm.

Upon return, contains an array of the average power of the carrier channel, relative to the peak power of the carrier channel, over the bandwidth indicated by the SEM Carrier IBW method. This value is expressed in dBm.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemResults.FetchLowerOffsetMarginArray(System.String,System.Double,NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemLowerOffsetMeasurementStatus[]@,System.Double[]@,System.Double[]@,System.Double[]@,System.Double[]@)
member.summary:
Returns an array of measurement status, margins, margin-frequencies, and absolute and relative powers corresponding to the margin-frequencies for lower offset segments. The relative powers are relative to the peak power in the carrier.
Use "segment(n)/chain(k)" as the selector string to read results from this method.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name, segment number, and chain number. If you do not specify the result name, the default result instance is used. Example:
"segment0/chain0"
"result::r1/segment0/chain0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.
Upon return, contains an array of lower (negative) offset segment measurement status, indicating whether the spectrum exceeds the SEM measurement mask limits in the lower offset segments.

Upon return, contains an array of margins from the SEM measurement mask for the lower offset. This value is expressed in dB. Margin is defined as the maximum difference between the spectrum and the mask.
Upon return, contains an array of frequencies corresponding to the margins for the lower (negative) offsets. This value is expressed in dB.
Upon return, contains an array of absolute powers corresponding to the margins for the lower offsets. This value is expressed in dBm.

Upon return, contains an array of relative powers corresponding to the margins for the lower offsets. The relative powers are relative to the peak power of the carrier channel. This value is expressed in dB.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

Upon return, contains the absolute power corresponding to the margin for the lower offset. This value is expressed in dBm.
Upon return, contains the relative power corresponding to the margin for the lower offset. The relative power is relative to the peak power of the carrier channel. This value is expressed in dB.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemResults.FetchLowerOffsetPower(System.String,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.niparametertypes:
System.String,System.Double,out System.Double,out System.Double,out System.Double,out
System.Double,out System.Double
member.summary:
Returns the total absolute and relative powers, peak absolute and relative powers and frequency at peak absolute power of lower offset segment. The relative power is relative to the peak power of the carrier.
Use "segment(n)/chain(k)/offset(l)" as the selector string to read results from this method.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name, segment number, chain number, and offset number. Example:
"segment0/chain0/offset0"
"result::r1/segment0/chain0/offset0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.
Upon return, contains the average power of the lower offset over the bandwidth determined by the offset start and stop frequencies. This value is expressed in dBm.
Upon return, contains the average power of the lower offset relative to the peak power of the carrier channel. This value is expressed in dB.
Upon return, contains the peak power of the lower offset. This value is expressed in dBm.
Upon return, contains the frequency at which the peak power occurs in the lower offset. This value is expressed in Hz.
Upon return, contains the peak power of the lower offset, relative to the peak power of the carrier channel. This value is expressed in dBm.
member.returns: Returns the status code of this method. The status code either indicates success or describes a warning condition.

member.name: M:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemResults.FetchUpperOffsetMargin(System.String,System.Double,NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemUpperOffsetMeasurementStatus@,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary:
Returns the measurement status, margin, margin-frequency, absolute and relative power corresponding to the margin-frequency for upper offset segment. The relative power is relative to the peak power in the carrier.
Use "segment(n)/chain(k)/offset(l)" as the selector string to read results from this method.
member.param:
Specifies a selector string comprising of the result name, segment number, chain number, and offset number. Example:
"segment0/chain0/offset0"
"result::r1/segment0/chain0/offset0"
You can use the
method to build the selector string.

Specifies the timeout for fetching the specified measurement. Set this value to an appropriate time, longer than expected for fetching the measurement. A value of -1 specifies that the method waits until the measurement is complete. This value is expressed in seconds.
Upon return, contains the upper (positive) offset segment measurement status, indicating whether the spectrum exceeds the SEM measurement mask limits in the upper offset segment.
Upon return, contains the margin from the SEM measurement mask for the upper offset. Margin is defined as the maximum difference between the spectrum and the mask. This value is expressed in dB.
Upon return, contains the frequency corresponding to the margin for the upper offset. This value is expressed in Hz.
Upon return, contains the power level of the spectrum, corresponding to the SEM Results Upper Offset Margin result. This value is expressed in dBm.

Upon return, contains the power level of the spectrum, corresponding to the
result. The power level is returned relative to the peak power of the carrier channel. This value is expressed in dB.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemSpanAuto
member.summary: Specifies whether the frequency range of the spectrum used for SEM measurement is computed automatically by the measurement or is configured by you.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemSpanAuto.False

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemSpanAuto.True

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemSweepTimeAuto.False
member.summary:
The measurement uses the sweep time that you specify in the
method.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemSweepTimeAuto.True
member.summary: The measurement automatically calculates the sweep time based on the standard and bandwidth you specify.

member.summary: The spectrum exceeds the SEM measurement mask and limits for the upper offset segment.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXSemUpperOffsetMeasurementStatus.Pass
member.summary: The spectrum does not exceed the SEM measurement mask and limits for the upper offset segment.

method to
, this parameter returns the mean of the average power computed for each averaging count.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccAveragingType
member.summary: Specifies the averaging type for the OFDMModAcc measurement.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccAveragingType.Rms
member.summary: The OFDMModAcc measurement is performed on I/Q data acquired in each averaging count. The scalar results and traces are linearly averaged over the averaging count.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccAveragingType.Vector
member.summary:
The acquired I/Q data is averaged across averaging count after aligning the data in time and phase using the
and
properties, respectively. The averaged I/Q data is used for the measurement. Note: You must ensure that the frequency reference is locked between the generator and the analyzer.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccVectorAveragingPhaseAlignmentEnabled
member.summary:
Specifies whether to enable phase alignment for the acquired I/Q data across multiple acquisitions. This method is considered only when you set the
method to True , when you set the
method to a value greater than 1, and when you set the
method to Vector . You can set this method to False when there is no phase offset between the acquired I/Q data of all averaging counts.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccVectorAveragingPhaseAlignmentEnabled.False
member.summary: Disables phase alignment for the acquired I/Q data across multiple acquisitions.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccVectorAveragingPhaseAlignmentEnabled.True
member.summary: Enables phase alignment for the acquired I/Q data across multiple acquisitions.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccVectorAveragingTimeAlignmentEnabled
member.summary:
Specifies whether to enable time alignment for the acquired I/Q data across multiple acquisitions. This method is considered only when you set the
method to True , when you set the
method to a value greater than 1, and when you set the
method to Vector . You can set this method to False when there is no time offset between the acquired I/Q data of all averaging counts.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccVectorAveragingTimeAlignmentEnabled.False
member.summary: Disables time alignment for the acquired I/Q data across multiple acquisitions.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccVectorAveragingTimeAlignmentEnabled.True
member.summary: Enables time alignment for the acquired I/Q data across multiple acquisitions.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccAutoLevelAllowOverflow
member.summary: Specifies whether the function should search for the optimum reference levels while allowing ADC overflow.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccAutoLevelAllowOverflow.False
member.summary: Disables searching for the optimum reference levels while allowing ADC overflow.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmModAccAutoLevelAllowOverflow.True
member.summary: Enables searching for the optimum reference levels while allowing ADC overflow.

member.name: T:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmEhtSigCompressionEnabled
member.summary: Enables EHT-SIG compression. This is applicable only for 802.11be MU PPDU signals.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmEhtSigCompressionEnabled.False
member.summary: Specifies that EHT-SIG compression is disabled.

member.name: F:NationalInstruments.RFmx.WlanMX.RFmxWlanMXOfdmEhtSigCompressionEnabled.True
member.summary: Specifies that EHT-SIG compression is enabled.

member.summary: Enables auto detection of phase rotation coefficients.
